ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product entry; no specific combo meal with fries and drink entry exists. Closest defensible anchor used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers fried chicken wings as the primary component; the combo meal (fries, dip, drink) is not separately codeable as a single FoodEx2 entry. Primary component code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for a fried chicken wings combo meal. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 set meal (6 wings, regular fries, ranch dip, drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal comprising six deep-fried skin-on chicken wings tossed in two chosen flavour sauces, one regular portion of potato fries, one ranch dipping sauce, and one carbonated soft drink, totalling approximately 650 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken wing, fried, coated (SR Legacy #05062) and Potatoes, french fried (SR Legacy #11400); composite adapted estimate for combo meal
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo entry covers this exact combo meal. Per-100g composite values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried coated chicken wings (FDC ID 171477; energy ~220 kcal, protein ~17 g, fat ~15 g, carbohydrate ~8 g per 100g) and french fries (FDC ID 170697), weighted by approximate component proportions in the 650 g serving (wings ~300 g, fries ~200 g, dip ~50 g, drink ~100 g). The resulting composite per-100g values (energy 207.69 kcal, protein 13.08 g, fat 13.85 g, carbohydrate 10.0 g) are within plausible range and consistent with the input record. Singapore HPB Foodo database does not carry an exact entry for this combo meal format. Values accepted at medium confidence.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for french fries (GI ~63) and refined-flour-coated fried chicken components.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific combo meal. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on dominant carbohydrate contributors: regular french fries (GI ~63, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care) and refined-flour-coated fried chicken (GI ~60–70 range per peer-reviewed analogues in the same publication). The carbonated sugar-sweetened soft drink component raises the effective glycaemic load. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (65 g, from 650 g × 10 g/100g) / 100 = 42.3. This represents a very high glycaemic load per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (492 mg/100g; approximately 3,200 mg per full 650 g serving) from seasoning, ranch dressing, and flavouring sauces, compounded by a sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this combo meal due to its very high sodium load per serving (approximately 3,200 mg, exceeding the 2,000 mg daily recommended limit in a single meal); if consumed, replace the soft drink with plain water, request minimal seasoning, and use ranch dip sparingly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycaemic load per serving (~42 GL units) driven by refined-flour-coated fried chicken, potato fries, and a sugar-sweetened carbonated soft drink, with minimal dietary fibre (0.77 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this combo meal; the combination of deep-fried refined-carbohydrate coating, high-GI potato fries, and a sugar-sweetened beverage produces a very high glycaemic load per serving that will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (3.08 g/100g), trans fat (0.08 g/100g), high sodium (492 mg/100g), and deep-frying-generated advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote vascular inflammation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this combo meal due to its combined burden of saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and pro-inflammatory AGEs from deep-frying; grilled skinless chicken with a non-fried side and water would be a substantially lower-risk alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs that worsen joint inflammation; sodium exceeds 400 mg/100g RA threshold; trans fat is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this combo meal; deep-fried preparation, high sodium, and trans fat collectively promote systemic inflammation and joint swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The 6 Piece Classic Chicken Wings Combo is a Western-style fast food set meal popular in Singapore, consisting of six skin-on chicken wings that are deep-fried in seasoned flour coating and tossed in two customer-chosen flavour sauces (such as buffalo, honey garlic, or BBQ), served alongside a regular portion of potato fries, a ranch dipping sauce, and a carbonated soft drink. The wings are crispy on the outside with juicy, flavourful meat inside, and the combo is designed as a shareable or individual indulgence meal. Nutritionally, the combo is energy-dense at approximately 208 kcal per 100g, with a full 650g serving delivering approximately 1,350 kcal. It is high in fat (13.85 g/100g), moderate in protein (13.08 g/100g), and contains refined carbohydrates from the flour coating and fries (10 g/100g). Sodium is notably high at 492 mg/100g (approximately 3,200 mg per serving), making it clinically significant for hypertension management. The glycaemic load per serving is very high (~42 GL units), driven by the fried coating, potato fries, and sugar-sweetened beverage, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Saturated fat (3.08 g/100g) and trans fat (0.08 g/100g) raise cardiovascular concern. The meal is halal-certified in Singapore fast food contexts. It is typically consumed as a lunch or dinner meal, often ordered via food delivery platforms. Clinical guidance consistently recommends replacing the soft drink with water, reducing seasoning, and requesting grilled alternatives for individuals with chronic disease risk.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 6 piece wings combo?
Sodium in fried chicken wings set meal
Can diabetics eat fried chicken wings?
Glycemic index of fried chicken wings
Is fried chicken wings combo suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this combo meal exists in the Singapore HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried coated chicken wings and french fries, weighted by approximate component proportions, and are consistent with the input record values. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed Atkinson et al. 2008 publication. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the composite nature of the meal and the absence of an exact approved-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite combo meal is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite combo meal are not reported in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Foodo for this preparation format. trans_fat_g is 0.08 g/100g (plausible for deep-fried commercial product; consistent with USDA FoodData Central fried chicken entries). All core macros (protein_g 13.08, fat_g 13.85, carbohydrate_g 10.0) are non-zero and nutritionally plausible for a fried chicken wings combo meal cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. The second portion entry in the input record ('1 set meal (8 wings, regular fries, ranch dip, 1 drink)') appears to be a data entry error as it lists 8 wings but the product is named '6 Piece'; this has been excluded from the serving definition and flagged for source review. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for french fries and peer-reviewed fried chicken analogues (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for this exact product exists. Nutrient source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy adapted composite; Singapore HPB Foodo does not carry an exact entry for this combo meal format.
